---
title: "About LASER"
page-layout: full
---

**LASER (Laboratory for Autonomous Systems Engineering Research)** is a research group in the Department of Chemical Engineering at Sunchon National University, Republic of Korea. The lab is led by [Prof. Kyung Hwan Ryu](/members.html).

Designing and operating complex engineered systems often relies on sustained expert experience and know-how across experiment design, operation, and decision-making. LASER asks a single question: how much of that reliance can be reduced through principled, verifiable computation? Our methods draw from sequential experiment design, hybrid surrogate modeling, uncertainty-aware decision engineering, and rigorous verification and assessment. We apply these across chemical, electrochemical, and bioprocess systems.

---

## Contact {#contact}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="38%"}
### Location

Department of Chemical Engineering, Sunchon National University
255 Jungang-ro, Suncheon, Jeollanam-do 57922, Republic of Korea

### Contact Information

- **Email**: khryu@scnu.ac.kr
- **Office**: D3-305
- **Phone**: +82 61-750-3588
:::

::: {.column width="4%"}
:::

::: {.column width="58%"}
<iframe
  src="https://www.google.com/maps?q=Sunchon+National+University,+255+Jungang-ro,+Suncheon,+Jeollanam-do,+Republic+of+Korea&output=embed&hl=en"
  width="100%" height="260" style="border:0; border-radius:6px;" allowfullscreen="" loading="lazy">
</iframe>
:::

::::

---

## Join Our Lab {#join-our-lab}

We are always looking for motivated people to join the team. Below are current openings.

### Open Positions

#### Researchers & Postdoctoral Fellows

We are looking for highly motivated researchers and postdoctoral candidates who are ready to lead independent research at the intersection of systems engineering and data intelligence. We especially value demonstrated expertise in one or more of the following areas:

- **Systems Engineering Leadership**: Leading high-fidelity modeling, multi-objective optimization, and advanced control strategies (e.g., MPC, RL) for complex process systems.
- **Robust Computational Methods**: Advancing Bayesian inference, robust optimization, and risk-aware decision frameworks applicable to real-world engineering uncertainty.
- **Physics-Informed Data Science**: Driving innovation in active learning, sequential experimental design, and physics-constrained machine learning for data-scarce engineering problems.

Innovation in systems engineering thrives on diverse perspectives. We are not limited to Chemical Engineering; we enthusiastically welcome applicants from any quantitative background—such as Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Data Science, or Biology—who are eager to apply their expertise to complex engineered systems.

#### Graduate Students (M.S. / Ph.D.)

We are looking for students curious about the intersection of Process Systems Engineering and Data Science. No prior research experience is required — we primarily look for intellectual curiosity, a solid mathematical or engineering foundation, and a genuine interest in solving problems through computation. Research topics include:

- **Autonomous Learning & Experimental Design**: Exploring data-efficient experimentation using active learning and Bayesian optimization to reduce experimental cost.
- **Process Design & Optimization under Uncertainty**: Applying and extending computational frameworks to design and optimize engineering systems with reliable, uncertainty-aware decision-making.
- **Process Monitoring & Hybrid Modeling**: Building expertise in physics-data hybrid modeling, process monitoring, and control strategies for real-world systems.

### How to Apply

Please send the following to **khryu@scnu.ac.kr**:

- CV or resume
- Transcript (for graduate applicants)
- Brief statement of research interests (1 page or less)
- Any relevant publications, reports, or research experience (if available)

---

::: {.callout-note}
## Prospective Graduate Students
For information about graduate admissions at Sunchon National University, please visit the [university website](https://www.scnu.ac.kr/neweng/main.do).
:::
